name: Mejoradad, Segurilla and Montesclaros – circular from Velada
name_original: Mejoradad, Segurilla y Montesclaros – circular desde Velada
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Velada traverse a region characterized by the flat expanse of the Toledo steppe and the natural environment of the Berrocal mountain range. The area features accessible routes, including the Vía Verde de la Jara, which offers a mostly flat, compacted earth surface suitable for touring bikes. This former railway line provides a consistent gradient through the Spanish countryside, connecting various towns. Best touring cycling routes around Velada The most popular touring cycling route is Talavera de la Reina, Alcaudete de la Jara, Calera y Chozas – circular on the Vía Verde de la Jara, a 44.0 miles (70.8 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 11 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Vía Verde de la Jara, known for its minimal inclines. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Mejoradad, Segurilla and Montesclaros – circular from Velada, a difficult 30.4 miles (48.9 km) path. This route features varied terrain and higher elevation gain, offering a more challenging experience. Local touring cyclists also love the Zamora Gate and Jail – Talavera de la Reina loop from Segurilla, a 17.1 miles (27.6 km) trail leading through historical areas, often completed in about 1 hour 59 minutes. Touring cycling around Velada is defined by the Vía Verde de la Jara, the Toledo steppe, and the Berrocal mountain range. The network offers options for different ability levels, from flat, easy paths to more challenging routes with greater elevation. The routes in Velada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 250 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Velada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Velada?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Velada, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. The region features a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging paths, with 2 easy routes and 20 moderate routes available.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Velada offers several easy routes perfect for families or beginners. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022697552/talavera-de-la-reina-alcaudete-de-la-jara-calera-y-chozas-circular-on-the-via-verde-de-la-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera de la Reina, Alcaudete de la Jara, Calera y Chozas – circular on the Vía Verde de la Jara&lt;/a&gt;. This route follows the Vía Verde de la Jara, which is known for its minimal inclines and compacted earth surface, making it very accessible.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes in Velada?
A: The terrain around Velada is diverse. Many routes, especially those on the Vía Verde de la Jara, feature a compacted earth surface with very gentle gradients, ideal for gravel or mountain bikes. Other routes, particularly those in the Berrocal mountain range, can involve varied terrain and higher elevation gains, offering a more challenging experience.

Q: What landmarks or natural features can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: You can explore a variety of landmarks and natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2552694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Jara&lt;/a&gt; itself is a significant feature, offering scenic views of the Spanish countryside. You might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2745111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Count’s Palace of Oropesa and Oropesa Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or natural beauty at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6116045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Portiña Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calera y Chozas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 12 difficult routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022700616/mejoradad-segurilla-and-montesclaros-circular-from-velada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mejoradad, Segurilla and Montesclaros – circular from Velada&lt;/a&gt;, which features varied terrain and significant elevation gain, providing a more demanding ride.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about cycling in Velada?
A: The touring cycling routes around Velada are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 250 touring cyclists have explored the area, often praising the accessible paths and the diverse landscape, from the flat Vía Verde to more challenging mountain routes.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Velada?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Velada are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022697552/talavera-de-la-reina-alcaudete-de-la-jara-calera-y-chozas-circular-on-the-via-verde-de-la-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera de la Reina, Alcaudete de la Jara, Calera y Chozas – circular on the Vía Verde de la Jara&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022700616/mejoradad-segurilla-and-montesclaros-circular-from-velada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mejoradad, Segurilla and Montesclaros – circular from Velada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Velada?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, regions like Velada in central Spain typically offer pleasant cycling conditions during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters might be cooler, so checking local weather forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Are there any routes that focus on historical areas?
A: Yes, some routes allow you to explore historical areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31877481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamora Gate and Jail – Talavera de la Reina loop from Segurilla&lt;/a&gt; leads through areas with historical significance, offering a blend of cycling and cultural exploration.

Q: What types of bikes are best suited for touring cycling in Velada?
A: Given the varied terrain, from compacted earth paths like the Vía Verde de la Jara to routes with higher elevation and potentially rougher surfaces, gravel bikes or mountain bikes are generally well-suited for touring cycling in Velada. These bikes offer the versatility and comfort needed for the region's diverse trails.

Q: Are there any routes with minimal elevation gain for an easier ride?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde de la Jara is particularly known for its minimal inclines, making routes that follow it ideal for those seeking an easier ride with low elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022697552/talavera-de-la-reina-alcaudete-de-la-jara-calera-y-chozas-circular-on-the-via-verde-de-la-jara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talavera de la Reina, Alcaudete de la Jara, Calera y Chozas – circular on the Vía Verde de la Jara&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example of such a route.
tips: The town is separated into two neighborhoods, one to the north and the other to the south, and a stream passes through the middle. The inhabitants of this town are proud of their Franciscan convent and the palace-house of Don Jiménez Dávila, Marquis of Velada. It has ideal land for agriculture (corn, potatoes, tobacco and its famous watermelon) and livestock (sheep, cows),

The caleros (firing stone in a circular kiln to make lime) have been for many years the only industry that provided work for the majority of the people in this town until the 1960s. The caleros are located on the Talavera road to the right and left of the road.
Very close to the center of the town of Montesclaros is the hermitage of San Sebastián with an interesting history and which, in turn, is surrounded by a very green and picturesque landscape.

waypoints.name: Casa-Palacio de Don Jiménez Dávila, Velada
